name: Jiangshi
description: The Jiangshi, also known as a Chinese hopping vampire or Chinese zombie, is a reanimated corpse in Chinese folklore. It is typically depicted as a stiff corpse dressed in official Qing dynasty attire, its arms outstretched, and it moves by hopping. The Jiangshi is created when a person's soul fails to leave the body due to improper burial, a violent death, or being possessed by evil spirits. Superstitions surrounding the Jiangshi are deeply rooted in Chinese culture, often used to explain sudden deaths or illnesses. These creatures are believed to absorb the 'qi' (life force) of living beings to sustain themselves, making them a dangerous presence. Their appearance is often grotesque, with pale or greenish skin, and they are said to be sensitive to light and mirrors. Folklore often includes methods to ward off Jiangshi, such as using sticky rice, mirrors, or Taoist magic. They represent fears surrounding death, the afterlife, and the importance of proper rituals in Chinese society, serving as cautionary tales against disrespecting the dead.
subtitle: Chinese Hopping Vampire
appearance: Jiangshi are typically depicted as stiff, decaying corpses dressed in the official garments of the Qing dynasty. They have pale or greenish skin, long fingernails, and blank, staring eyes. Their limbs are stiff, forcing them to hop instead of walk. A paper talisman is often attached to their forehead.
habitat: Jiangshi are said to inhabit dark and isolated places, such as abandoned buildings, graveyards, and caves. They are often found near where they died or were buried.
behavior: Jiangshi are primarily driven by instinct to feed on the 'qi' (life force) of living beings. They move by hopping and attempt to drain the life force of anyone they encounter. They are generally mindless and follow simple patterns of movement, often attracted to the scent of living beings or loud noises.

keyMyths.title: Improper Burial
keyMyths.description: One common origin story involves improper burial rites preventing the soul from leaving the body, leading to reanimation. The lack of proper closure causes the corpse to rise as a Jiangshi.

keyMyths.title: Violent Death
keyMyths.description: A violent or traumatic death can also trap the soul within the body, resulting in the creation of a Jiangshi. The unresolved trauma binds the spirit to its physical form.

keyMyths.title: Evil Spirits
keyMyths.description: Possession by evil spirits is another explanation for the creation of Jiangshi. Malevolent entities can animate a corpse and use it for their own purposes.
